methodology: STRIDE threat model; DREAD risk scoring; ISO/SAE 21434 §15 attack path analysis

threat_scenarios.threat: Malformed frame injection via RELAY adapter send()
threat_scenarios.attack_path: Attacker supplies JSON Message with out-of-range id or oversized payload
countermeasures: from_message validates protocol == Lin (3)
validate_frame enforces id ≤ 0x3F and len ≤ 8
REQ-SEC-001, REQ-SEC-008

threat_scenarios.threat: Checksum bypass — attacker forges checksum field in Message meta
threat_scenarios.attack_path: Attacker supplies meta[lin.checksum] = arbitrary value in from_message
countermeasures: from_message parses checksum from meta for Frame reconstruction only
send_header recomputes checksum before broadcast
validate_frame called before broadcast

threat_scenarios.attack_path: Attacker subscribes with depth=1 and DropNewest to cause frame loss
countermeasures: Back-pressure never blocks send_header (non-blocking push)
Metrics track dropped frame counts
REQ-LIN-017

threat_scenarios.threat: Race condition in concurrent send_header calls
threat_scenarios.attack_path: Two concurrent callers both succeed and broadcast different frames for same id
countermeasures: BusInner protected by tokio::sync::Mutex
Concurrent safety integration test (8 tasks, 16 frames each)

threat_scenarios.attack_path: Malicious or vulnerable version of a dependency crate introduced
countermeasures: cargo audit on every CI run
Cargo.lock committed (reproducible builds)
Dependabot alerts enabled

threat_scenarios.threat: E2E CRC forgery — attacker crafts message with valid CRC over malicious payload
threat_scenarios.attack_path: Attacker with knowledge of DataID/SourceID computes correct CRC-16/CCITT-FALSE over forged payload and injects via RELAY bus
countermeasures: CRC is a data integrity mechanism, not an authentication mechanism
DataID and SourceID serve as weak MAC; attacker must know both
REQ-SAFETY-005: CRC-16/CCITT-FALSE detected in 1/65536 brute-force attempts
Full authentication requires an upper-layer MAC (out of scope for this SEOOC component)
threat_scenarios.residual_risk: Medium — integrator MUST add MAC for authenticated safety data (SEOOC assumption REQ-SEOOC-008)

threat_scenarios.threat: E2E sequence counter replay — attacker replays previously valid protected message
threat_scenarios.attack_path: Attacker captures a protect()ed message with seq=N and replays it after the receiver has advanced past N
countermeasures: REQ-SAFETY-009: Receiver::unwrap returns SequenceGap for non-monotonic counter
REQ-SEC-003: counter replay is detected and rejected
threat_scenarios.residual_risk: Low — requires local bus capture capability; detected by SequenceGap

threat_scenarios.threat: LDF file injection — malicious LDF file with crafted frame IDs
threat_scenarios.attack_path: Attacker replaces LDF file on disk or in a build system with one containing out-of-range frame IDs (e.g., 0x40) or extremely long signal names
countermeasures: REQ-LDF-014: parse() does not panic on malformed or hostile input
REQ-SEOOC-006: integrator validates LDF-derived IDs before use
REQ-SEC-004: LDF parse is fuzz-resistant
threat_scenarios.residual_risk: Negligible — ID validation at MasterNode level provides defence-in-depth

threat_scenarios.threat: Non-LIN protocol message injected as LIN frame via RELAY adapter
threat_scenarios.attack_path: Attacker sends relay::Message with protocol = 1 (CAN) to a rust-lin Node; from_message might construct a Frame
countermeasures: REQ-ADAPT-002: from_message checks msg.protocol == Protocol::Lin (3)
REQ-SEC-002: from_message returns Err(InvalidFrame) for any other protocol

threat_scenarios.threat: High-frequency master sends saturate subscriber queues
threat_scenarios.attack_path: MasterNode schedule runs with delay_ms=0; 1000+ frames/sec overwhelm subscriber queues with back-pressure
countermeasures: REQ-SEC-007: rate_limit_per_sec drops excess frames before delivery
Back-pressure policy is caller-configurable (DropNewest / DropOldest)

threat_scenarios.threat: Stale slave response persistence after deregistration
threat_scenarios.attack_path: Attacker causes SlaveNode::set_response(id, None) to fail silently; old response persists and is returned by master
countermeasures: REQ-SLAVE-003: set_response(None) calls Bus::publish(id, None) atomically
Bus::publish(id, None) removes response from BusInner.responses map
Unit test verifies NoResponse is returned after deregistration

threat_scenarios.threat: Memory safety violation via unsafe code introduction
threat_scenarios.attack_path: Contributor adds unsafe block that causes UB on adversarial input
countermeasures: REQ-SEC-006: no unsafe code in library or binary
rsfusa lint checks for unsafe blocks on every CI run
cargo clippy -D warnings enforced in CI
threat_scenarios.residual_risk: Negligible — Rust ownership model prevents spatial/temporal safety violations
